title: Execution Outcome Verification Receipt
description: JSON Schema for an EOV execution receipt as defined in draft-morrow-sogomonian-exec-outcome-attest-00. An execution receipt is a signed, bound claim about a specific execution event. It records what the acting system claims happened (not merely that an invocation occurred), signed at execution time, and structured for independent verification by a party not present at execution.

schema_version.description: Schema version identifier. Must be 'eov/v1' for this version.

invocation_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this invocation event. MUST be generated by the acting system at invocation time and MUST NOT be reused. UUID v4 recommended.

invocation_context.description: Binding context: who invoked the action, under what delegation, with what inputs.

invoking_principal.description: Identifier of the entity that authorized this invocation. For AIP-aware deployments, this SHOULD be the agent's AIP agent_id from the AAT. For non-AIP deployments, a URI or DID identifying the acting principal is acceptable.

delegation_token.description: Optional. A reference or hash of the delegation credential authorizing this invocation. For HDP (Human Delegation Provenance) compatible deployments, this field anchors the HDP record into the receipt. For AIP deployments, this is the AAT identifier or a hash of the AAT payload.

tool_name.description: The name of the tool, function, or action that was invoked. For MCP deployments, this is the tools/call method name. MUST be a stable identifier (not a display string).

inputs_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical serialization of the invocation parameters (inputs). Format: 'sha256:<hex>'. The inputs themselves are not stored in the receipt; only their hash. This allows the receipt to be compact while still enabling a verifier who holds the inputs to confirm they match.

invocation_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the invocation was initiated.

context_extensions.description: Optional map of additional context fields. Deployment-specific. MUST NOT contradict any other invocation_context fields.

outcome_claim.description: What the acting system claims happened. This is the semantic core of the receipt: it distinguishes an execution receipt from a mere invocation log.

status.description: Terminal status of the execution. 'partial' indicates the action began but did not complete fully. 'denied' indicates the action was blocked before execution (e.g., by a policy engine such as AIP Layer 2).

outputs_hash.description: Optional. SHA-256 hash of the canonical serialization of the execution outputs. Required when status is 'success' or 'partial'. Not present when status is 'denied' or when no output was produced.

completion_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the execution completed (or was terminated).

outcome_detail.description: Optional. Human-readable or machine-parseable summary of the outcome. For 'failure' or 'partial' statuses, SHOULD include the error class and a brief description. MUST NOT contain sensitive data (credentials, PII, secrets).

side_effects.description: Optional. List of identifiable side effects produced by this execution (e.g., records written, files modified, external calls made). Each entry is a URI or structured identifier.

outcome_extensions.description: Optional map of additional outcome fields. Deployment-specific.

signer_identity.description: Optional but RECOMMENDED. Identifies the signing key and its binding to the acting entity's Layer 1 attestation.

key_id.description: Identifier for the signing key (e.g., JWK thumbprint, key fingerprint, or registry reference).

attestation_ref.description: Optional reference to the Layer 1 attestation that binds this key to the acting entity (e.g., a RATS conceptual message, an AIP registry entry URI, or a SPIFFE SVID).

receipt_signature.description: Base64url-encoded cryptographic signature over the canonical serialization of all other fields in this receipt (excluding receipt_signature itself). The signing algorithm SHOULD be Ed25519 or ES256. The signature binds the outcome_claim to the invocation_context and prevents post-hoc modification.

receipt_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the receipt was produced and signed. This MAY differ from invocation_context.invocation_timestamp (receipt may be produced slightly after completion). MUST NOT precede outcome_claim.completion_timestamp.

transparency_log_ref.description: Optional. Reference to a SCITT or compatible transparency log entry where this receipt was anchored. Presence of this field indicates the receipt has been submitted to an independent log.

log_id.description: Identifier for the transparency log (e.g., the log's public key fingerprint or a well-known URI).

entry_id.description: Log-specific entry identifier or hash (e.g., SCITT receipt identifier).

submission_timestamp.description: Timestamp at which the receipt was submitted to the log.
